questions.original_question: 路特斯科技在2024年进入了哪些新市场？
questions.answer:
Lotus Technology 在 2024 年进入了多个新市场。具体来说，Eletre 被交付到了海湾合作委员会地区、新西兰、印度和埃及，随后还开始接受澳大利亚、日本和韩国的生产订单。此外，Emeya 在欧洲开始交付，并在海湾合作委员会地区、日本、韩国和东南亚接受预订。这些市场的扩展进一步推动了 Lotus Technology 的全球布局。

以上信息基于2024年11月12日之前的知识。
rewritten_question: Which new markets did Lotus Technology enter in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.
rag_info.sub_query: Which new markets did Lotus Technology enter in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: With a global sales network of over 200 stores in prime locations around the world, Lotus Technology continues to leverage its global distribution footprint for international expansion. After China and Europe, Lotus Technology began taking orders for the Eletre in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas in the first quarter. Lotus Technology also expects to begin expanding Emeya sales to new markets in the third quarter of the year. "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence, Lotus Technology is making steady progress on delivering Lotus Tech’s Vision80 plan," said Lotus Technology CEO Qingfeng Feng. "Lotus Technology's global strategy continues to drive growth and success as the company expands its presence and sales across key luxury markets worldwide. Lotus Technology is encouraged by the positive feedback received in these new markets and looks forward to rolling out new models to new geographies throughout the year."
rag_info.sub_query: Which new markets did Lotus Technology enter in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.
rag_info.sub_query: Which new markets did Lotus Technology enter in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content:
Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$173 million and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered approximately 2,180 vehicles, with global markets outside China contributing over 75% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,180 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where Lotus Technology operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.
rag_info.sub_query: Which new markets did Lotus Technology enter in 2024?

rag_info.chunk_content:
Robust start in 2024, marking the strongest first half-year performance in Lotus Technology's history.  
Balanced global allocation, with strong US deliveries contributing after the brand’s re-entry into the market.  
Launched bespoke services and continued growing technology-related income, which are expected to be potential contributors to profit margin in the future.  
Adjusted the annual delivery target due to near-term disruptions from trade uncertainties and tariff hikes.  
Initiated the “Win26” Plan aiming to achieve positive operating cash flow and EBITDA in 2026. New York – August 28, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the second quarter and half year ended June 30, 2024.

(“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Ten Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024, achieving a total of 8,631 vehicles delivered. The primary markets driving these deliveries were Europe and China, which accounted for approximately 35% and 25% of total deliveries, respectively. In October, the company launched an open beta test for its urban Navigate on Autopilot (NOA) feature in key cities and introduced several over-the-air (OTA) intelligent driving functions in Europe. 

Additionally, Lotus Robotics, the company's division focused on intelligent driving solutions, is providing services not only for Lotus vehicles but also for other major automotive manufacturers, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. The company has also been recognized for its commitment to sustainability, receiving the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category, making it the only automotive industry recipient of this accolade.

It is important to note that the delivery figures are based on invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the U.S. market, and may differ slightly from the company's financial accounting records. Overall, Lotus Technology continues to make significant strides in vehicle delivery, technological advancements, and sustainability initiatives.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Over 12,000 vehicles delivered in 2024, a year-on-year increase of over 70%. Total revenue reached $924 million, a 36% year-on-year growth. Achieved a reduction in operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through rigorous execution of efficiency enhancement initiatives. Lotus Technology is expected to consolidate the entire global business of the Lotus brand by acquisition of a majority interest in Lotus UK upon the closing of Gee. New York – April 22, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter
and Full Year 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions, achieved significant milestones by delivering over 12,000 vehicles, which represents a remarkable year-on-year increase of more than 70%. The company's total revenue reached $924 million, reflecting a 36% growth from the previous year. Furthermore, it successfully reduced operating expenses for five consecutive quarters through various efficiency enhancement initiatives. Looking to the future, Lotus plans to strengthen its global presence by acquiring a majority interest in Lotus UK following the completion of the Gee transaction. The unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the full year ending December 31, 2024, were announced on April 22, 2025, in New York.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – January 14, 2025 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology Inc. also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology Inc. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Preliminary Delivery
Results of 2024 
summary: On January 14, 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving an impressive total of 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting a year-on-year growth of over 70%, the highest among luxury brands. Notably, around 25% of these deliveries were made in China. Looking ahead to 2025, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20%, with China expected to contribute 25-28% to this growth. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including its iconic LOTUS branding, ensuring consistent global representation. Additionally, the delivery figures reported for the US market are based on company business unit reports and may differ slightly from financial accounting records. Lotus is positioned alongside other prestigious luxury brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, and Rolls Royce, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and performance in the automotive industry.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first eleven months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. delivered 9,871 vehicles in the first eleven months of 2024. As a top-selling British luxury brand, Lotus continued its strong momentum in the European market, which contributed nearly 40% of total deliveries for the eleven months ended November 30, 2024. China, as a new and growing market for Lotus, contributed about 25% of the total deliveries. In November, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Green Apple Environment Award for its commitment to sustainable environmental practices. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by the Company. Note 2: Including Bentley Motors Limited, Rolls-Royce Motor Cars, Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc, and McLaren Automotive Limited.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Announces Delivery Updates
of First Eleven Months of 2024 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a key player in the intelligent and luxury mobility sector, announced its vehicle delivery results for the first eleven months of 2024 on November 12, revealing a total of 9,871 vehicles delivered. The British luxury brand showcased strong performance in Europe, which represented nearly 40% of its deliveries, while China emerged as a significant market, contributing around 25%. In recognition of its commitment to sustainability, Lotus received the Green Apple Environment Award in November. The delivery figures include invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, with potential slight variations from the company's financial records. The announcement also referenced other luxury automotive brands like Bentley, Rolls Royce, Aston Martin, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to affluent consumers seeking exclusivity in their vehicles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited
Second Quarter and First Half 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In the first half of 2024, the brand experienced its best performance to date, driven by a well-balanced global strategy and significant deliveries in the US after its market re-entry. The introduction of tailored services and growth in technology-related revenue are expected to improve profit margins in the future. However, the annual delivery target has been revised due to short-term challenges from trade uncertainties and tariff hikes. Additionally, the brand has launched the "Win26" Plan, which aims for positive operating cash flow and EBITDA by 2026. Lotus Technology Inc. also reported its unaudited financial results for the second quarter and the first half of 2024, highlighting its position in the global intelligent and luxury mobility sector, although specific financial details were not disclosed.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited
Second Quarter and First Half 2024 Financial Results 
summary: In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. reported its strongest performance to date, bolstered by a well-balanced global allocation and significant deliveries in the US following its market re-entry. The company anticipates that the introduction of bespoke services and growth in technology-related income will further enhance profit margins. However, it has adjusted its annual delivery target due to short-term challenges related to trade uncertainties and tariff increases. Additionally, the brand has initiated the "Win26" Plan, which aims to achieve positive operating cash flow and EBITDA by 2026. The unaudited financial results for the second quarter and the first half of 2024, ending June 30, were disclosed in a report released on August 28, 2024, highlighting Lotus's position as a leading global provider of intelligent and luxury mobility solutions.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

metadata.title_summary:
title: SIGNATURES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has filed a report in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, officially signed by an authorized representative. On January 14, 2025, the company announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for 2024, achieving a significant milestone with 12,065 vehicles delivered, reflecting over 70% year-on-year growth. This performance established Lotus as the fastest-growing luxury brand, with approximately 25% of deliveries originating from China. Looking ahead, Lotus aims to increase global deliveries by 20% in 2025, with China's contribution expected to rise to 25-28%. The company has also regained proprietary rights to its trademarks, including the iconic LOTUS roundel and the Chinese character for Lotus ("莲花"), reinforcing its global branding consistency. The reported delivery figures encompass invoiced and commissioned deliveries in the US, based on submissions from various business units, and may differ slightly from the company's financial records. Additionally, the summary highlights the competitive landscape, referencing prestigious luxury automotive brands such as Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren, known for their exceptional craftsmanship and appeal to elite car enthusiasts.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

metadata.title_summary:
title: A. Operating Results 
summary: We are a leading global provider of luxury mobility solutions, focusing on the design and development of sophisticated lifestyle vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." Our product lineup includes a comprehensive range of Lotus-branded cars, reflecting our commitment to quality in the luxury automotive sector. In 2024, we achieved significant milestones, delivering 12,134 vehicles, including both lifestyle and sports models, through our distribution partner LTIL. Our workforce expanded to 1,996 full-time employees, with approximately 52% dedicated to Research and Development across teams in China and Europe. Financially, we experienced substantial revenue growth, with total revenues increasing from US$9.6 million in 2022 to US$924.3 million in 2024. However, this growth was accompanied by net losses, which escalated from US$724.6 million in 2022 to US$1,107.3 million in 2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology Inc.'s business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by the company.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million, reflecting an 811% year-on-year growth and a gross profit margin of 18%. The company delivered 2,180 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. This quarter marked the launch of the Emeya, Lotus's first electric hyper-grand tourer, and the introduction of the Chapman Bespoke service, which offers premium customization options for its vehicles. The company's direct-to-customer sales model in China led to over 100% growth in deliveries, while the Emira, a traditional sports car, began deliveries in the US. With a robust global sales network of over 200 stores, Lotus Tech is expanding its reach into new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas, and plans to introduce additional models throughout the year. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to innovation and its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy in driving growth in key luxury markets.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With a global sales network of over 200 stores in prime locations around the world, Lotus Technology continues to leverage its global distribution footprint for international expansion. After China and Europe, Lotus Technology began taking orders for the Eletre in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas in the first quarter. Lotus Technology also expects to begin expanding Emeya sales to new markets in the third quarter of the year. "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence, Lotus Technology is making steady progress on delivering Lotus Tech’s Vision80 plan," said Lotus Technology CEO Qingfeng Feng. "Lotus Technology's global strategy continues to drive growth and success as the company expands its presence and sales across key luxury markets worldwide. Lotus Technology is encouraged by the positive feedback received in these new markets and looks forward to rolling out new models to new geographies throughout the year."

Lotus Technology revealed luxury customized editions of classic sports cars and battery electric vehicle lifestyle models. New York  
– May 29, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or the "Company") (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. achieved total revenue of \$173 million in the quarter, a year-on-year growth of 811% and a gross profit margin of 18%, powered by an asset-light model and high-margin businesses including research and development services and accessories. Lotus Technology successfully launched its Chapman Bespoke service in the first quarter of 2024, providing premium customization to Lotus vehicles. Lotus Technology delivered a total of 2,180 vehicles in the first quarter. The Company began deliveries of the Emeya in China, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, and further expanded its product portfolio to four models. In the China market where Lotus Technology operates a direct-to-customer sales model, the deliveries in the first quarter had a year-on-year growth of over 100%. Customer deliveries of the Emira, the brand’s internal combustion engine sports car manufactured in the UK, also commenced in the US at the beginning of this year. Global markets outside China contributed over 75% of Lotus Technology's deliveries in the first quarter.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology
Reports Unaudited First Quarter 2024 Financial Result 
summary: Lotus Tech, a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, reported impressive financial results for the first quarter of 2024, with revenues reaching $173 million—a remarkable 811% increase year-on-year. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 18%, supported by its asset-light model and high-margin sectors like R&D services and accessories. During this period, Lotus delivered 2,180 vehicles, with over 75% of these sales occurring in global markets outside of China. The company launched its first electric hyper-grand tourer, the Emeya, and expanded its product lineup to four models, while also beginning customer deliveries of the Emira sports car in the U.S. 

Lotus Tech's direct-to-customer sales model in China saw over 100% growth in deliveries compared to the previous year. The company is leveraging its extensive global sales network, which includes over 200 stores, to facilitate international growth and has begun taking orders for the Eletre model in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas. CEO Qingfeng Feng emphasized the company's commitment to its Vision80 plan, highlighting the effectiveness of its global strategy and expressing optimism about the positive reception in new markets and the launch of additional models throughout the year. Additionally, Lotus introduced its Chapman Bespoke service, offering premium customization options for its vehicles, further enhancing its luxury appeal.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With a global sales network of over 200 stores in prime locations around the world, Lotus Technology continues to leverage its global distribution footprint for international expansion. After China and Europe, Lotus Technology began taking orders for the Eletre in more than 10 new markets across the Middle East, Asia, and the Americas in the first quarter. Lotus Technology also expects to begin expanding Emeya sales to new markets in the third quarter of the year. "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence, Lotus Technology is making steady progress on delivering Lotus Tech’s Vision80 plan," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng. "Lotus Technology's global strategy continues to drive growth and success as Lotus Technology expands its presence and sales across key luxury markets worldwide. Lotus Technology is encouraged by the positive feedback received in these new markets and looks forward to rolling out new models to new geographies throughout the year."

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Tech and  L  Catterton Asia Acquisition
Corp Announce Closing of Business Combination 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: With both BEVs already on the roads and two more fully electric models planned for launch over the next two years, Lotus Technology Inc. is on track to become the first traditional luxury automotive brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. Since the transaction was announced, Lotus Technology Inc. has raised more than US$880 million in pre-closing and private investment in public equity (“PIPE”) financing commitments from global investors, existing shareholders, and strategic partners, representing one of the largest amounts of additional financings raised in connection with a de-SPAC transaction since 2023. “Lotus Technology Inc. is thrilled to announce its upcoming debut on the Nasdaq as it completes its business combination with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Technology Inc. “This is a pivotal moment in our journey. Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to accelerating its growth as a listed company, leading the electric transformation of the global luxury battery electric vehicle market together with L Catterton.” “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp's partnership with Lotus Technology Inc. has gotten stronger over time and our respect for the mission Lotus Technology Inc. is on has only increased,” said Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L CAA. “We are excited about the road ahead as we continue building the definitive luxury electric vehicle brand together, leveraging our global consumer insights and our strategic relationship with LVMH.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - February 23, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, rang today’s opening bell at the Nasdaq stock exchange in New York City in celebration of its public listing. The Company’s American depositary shares (“ADSs”) commenced trading today on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) under the ticker symbol “LOT” following the completion of its business combination on February 22, 2024, with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”), a special purpose acquisition company (“SPAC”) formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm. Lotus Technology Inc. CEO Feng Qingfeng was joined by members of the Lotus Technology Inc. and L Catterton teams in New York City to witness this important milestone, which will help Lotus Technology Inc. to further its mission of becoming an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. “This is an exciting moment for all of us at Lotus Technology Inc.,” said Mr. Feng. “I would like to extend my appreciation to our shareholders, partners, suppliers, our outstanding employees, and most importantly, our customers, for their support.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. is proud to carry Lotus’s heritage into the future and to open a new chapter in the Lotus story with our public listing today.” Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, said: “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp believes that Lotus Technology Inc. is ideally positioned to seize the tremendous opportunity in the fast-growing and underserved luxury electric vehicle market, leveraging our global consumer insights and strategic relationship with LVMH. Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

● Following Southeast Asia deliveries, the Eletre (SUV) model started deliveries to new markets such as the GCC, New Zealand, India, and Egypt. ● Production orders for the Eletre (SUV) model began for new markets such as Australia, Japan, and Korea. ● The Emeya (GT, Sedan) model started deliveries in Europe. ● Reservations for the Emeya (GT, Sedan) model began in the GCC, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia.   
● Lotus Robotics provides solutions to multiple OEMs: Lotus Robotics, a subsidiary for intelligent automobility technologies development, licenses several leading automakers to provide them with its intelligent driving technology and solutions, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto.   
● Strategic capital raised: As previously announced, in June 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a convertible note purchase agreement with an aggregate principal amount of approximately \$110 million through a private placement from its existing strategic shareholder.   
● Dual Red Dot Awards: In June 2024, two Red Dot Awards in Product Design were given to the groundbreaking hyper electric vehicles: the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan).   
● Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 Nomination: In July 2024, Lotus Technology was nominated as a finalist in the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the business transformation and Net Zero: operations transformation. Reuters Sustainability Awards are the world’s leading awards recognizing leadership in sustainable business.

metadata.title_summary:
title: ● Delivery Updates: 
summary: Lotus has broadened the reach of its Eletre SUV, launching deliveries in new markets such as the GCC, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, while also starting production orders for Australia, Japan, and Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has begun its rollout in Europe, with reservations now available in the GCC, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. Additionally, Lotus Robotics, a subsidiary dedicated to intelligent automobility technologies, is supplying solutions to various OEMs, including Lynk&Co and Farizon Auto. In June 2024, the company secured around $110 million through a convertible note purchase agreement with a strategic shareholder. Lotus also earned two Red Dot Awards for Product Design for its Eletre and Emeya models. Moreover, in July 2024, Lotus Tech was recognized as a finalist for the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024, highlighting its commitment to business transformation and achieving Net Zero operations.

metadata.title_summary:
title: ● Delivery Updates: 
summary: Lotus has made significant strides in expanding its vehicle delivery and production capabilities, launching the Eletre SUV in new markets such as the GCC, New Zealand, India, and Egypt, while also initiating production for Australia, Japan, and Korea. The Emeya GT sedan has commenced deliveries in Europe, with reservations now available in the GCC, Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia. all_retrieved_content.page_content: At no time prior to or after its termination did Credit Suisse indicate that it had any specific concerns with the Business Combination, and Credit Suisse did not advise Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors that it was in any dispute or disagreement with Lotus Technology Corporation or Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors, any matter relating to the operations, policies, procedures or practices of Lotus Technology Corporation or Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors or the contents of this proxy statement/prospectus or the registration statement of which it forms a part. Credit Suisse further confirmed in its resignation letter that its resignation as the exclusive equity capital markets advisor to Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors is not the result of any dispute or disagreement with Lotus Technology Corporation or Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors or any matter relating to Lotus Technology Corporation’s or Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors’ operations, prospects, policies, procedures or practices. On October 16, 2023, Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors and Credit Suisse entered into the CMA Termination Letter, pursuant to which, effective as of the same day, (a) Credit Suisse ceased to act and no longer acts in any capacity, role or relationship as contemplated under its engagement letter as the exclusive equity capital markets advisor to Lotus Capital Acquisition Advisors and in any other capacity, role or relationship in connection with the Business Combination;

metadata.title_summary:
title: Termination of CS’s Engagements and Waiver of Deferred Underwriting Commission 
summary: Credit Suisse (CS) has officially terminated its roles as the joint placement agent for LTC and as the exclusive equity capital markets advisor for LCAA, effective October 12, 2023. This decision, communicated via email to LTC's CFO, Mr. Alexious Kuen Long Lee, and followed by a formal resignation to LCAA, is attributed to CS's merger with UBS, which resulted in a resource reallocation that hindered CS's ability to continue its engagements. Importantly, CS clarified that its resignation was not due to any disputes or operational issues with either LTC or LCAA.

The termination of CS's engagements signifies a notable shift in the partnership dynamics, prompting LTC and LCAA to potentially seek alternative agents or strategies for future placements. Despite the cessation of these roles, certain provisions from the engagement letters, such as confidentiality and indemnification obligations, will remain in effect. CS has waived its right to any fees associated with private placements and deferred underwriting commissions, indicating a strategic realignment rather than a reaction to conflicts.

While LTC and LCAA do not anticipate that CS's resignation will negatively impact the proposed Business Combination or PIPE Financing, there is concern that market perception could be adversely affected. If investor sentiment worsens, it may lead to shareholder opposition or redemption of shares, which could jeopardize the transaction's completion. CS has distanced itself from the proxy statement/prospectus related to the Business Combination, disclaiming responsibility for any associated communications and advising shareholders not to rely on its previous involvement.

Overall, the termination of CS's engagements marks a significant change in the advisory landscape for LTC and LCAA, with potential implications for their future capital market strategies and stakeholder perceptions.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On November 29, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a share buyback agreement, or the Meritz Share Buyback Agreement with Meritz, pursuant to which Meritz agrees to sell and surrender, and Lotus Technology agrees to repurchase, 17,500,000 ADSs, or the Repurchased Meritz ADSs. Ordinary Shares underlying the Repurchased Meritz ADSs constitute a part of Meritz Investment, or the Meritz Shares. The total consideration payable by Lotus Technology for the buyback equals the amount that provides Meritz with a 12.5% internal rate of return, calculated for the period between the Closing Date and the date of the closing of the buyback, on US$175 million. Lotus Technology did not repurchase the 17,500,000 ADSs under the Meritz Share Buyback Agreement by December 31, 2024, and Lotus Technology entered into an amended and restated share buyback agreement, or the A&R Meritz Share Buyback Agreement with Meritz and Lotus EV Limited, Lotus Technology's wholly-owned subsidiary, on March 28, 2025, to amend and restate in its entirety the Meritz Share Buyback Agreement. Pursuant to the A&R Meritz Share Buyback Agreement, Meritz agrees to sell, and Lotus Technology agrees to repurchase, 17,500,000 ADSs. Ordinary Shares underlying the repurchased ADSs constitute a part of the Meritz Investment.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (vi) Subsequent Agreements 
summary: On November 29, 2024, our company entered into the Meritz Share Buyback Agreement to repurchase 17,500,000 American Depositary Shares (ADSs) for a total of $175 million, ensuring Meritz a 12.5% internal rate of return (IRR). However, the buyback was not finalized by the end of 2024, prompting the signing of an amended agreement on March 28, 2025. This new agreement maintained the original terms and required a deposit of $150,937,500 to be paid by April 30, 2025, with the remaining balance calculated to ensure the same 12.5% IRR on $48,109,420, to be settled by September 30, 2025. The completion of the buyback is subject to standard conditions, and upon its conclusion, Meritz will return the ADSs in exchange for payment.

The amended agreement also suspended certain obligations under the Meritz Subscription Agreement, including the requirement to maintain $175 million in unrestricted cash and to deposit additional cash as collateral due to ADS price fluctuations. Furthermore, Meritz agreed not to enforce $325 million in U.S. treasury bonds pledged by the company. If the buyback does not close by September 30, 2025, an event of default will be triggered unless specific conditions are met by Meritz. In such a case, Meritz would transfer 12,689,058 ADSs to Lotus EV Limited, which would then pledge these shares back to Meritz, allowing for immediate enforcement. Additionally, Meritz could enforce security under the Subscription Agreement and act on 7,000,000 Ordinary Shares pledged by Lotus Advanced Technology Limited Partnership, with the right to terminate the amended buyback agreement.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: (i) Merger Transaction On February 22, 2024, the Merger Transaction was completed as disclosed in note 1(b). (ii) Private Investment in Public Equity (PIPE) Investments On February 15, 2024, Lotus Group entered into a new Private Investment in Public Equity (PIPE) investment agreement with an investor, as disclosed in note 13. (iii) New grants of share options From January 1, 2024, to March 31, 2024, Lotus Technology granted an aggregated number of 5,972,760 share options to employees pursuant to the 2022 Share Incentive Plan, at a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per share option. (iv) Loans to a related party On March 11, 2024, Lotus Technology's subsidiary and a related party, ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder, entered into a loan agreement, agreeing that Lotus Technology's subsidiary will provide unsecured loans with a total principal amount of GBP 275,000 (equivalent to US$354,000) with a term of 5 years and an annual interest rate of 6.95%.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 30. SUBSEQUENT EVENTS 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Subsequent Events**

The chapter on Subsequent Events outlines several significant developments impacting the Company following the reporting period ending December 31, 2023. 

1. **Merger Transaction:** A key event was the completion of a merger transaction on February 22, 2024, as detailed in note 1(b). This strategic move is expected to enhance the Company's market position and operational capabilities.

2. **PIPE Investments:** On February 15, 2024, the Group entered into a new PIPE (Private Investment in Public Equity) investment agreement with an investor, as noted in note 13. This investment is anticipated to provide additional capital to support the Company's growth initiatives.

3. **Share Options Granted:** Between January 1, 2024, and March 31, 2024, the Company granted a total of 5,972,760 share options to employees under the 2022 Share Incentive Plan. The options have a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per option, aligning employee interests with the Company's performance.

4. **Loans to Related Party:** On March 11, 2024, the Company’s subsidiary entered into a loan agreement with a related party controlled by the Controlling Shareholder. This agreement involves providing unsecured loans totaling GBP 275,000 (approximately US$354,000) for a term of 5 years at an annual interest rate of 6.95%. This transaction raises considerations regarding related party transactions and potential conflicts of interest.

5. **Termination of Pledge:** In April 2024, the Company terminated the pledge related to the land use right valued at US$32,653 and corporate buildings under construction valued at US$37,287. These assets were previously included in the operating lease right-of-use assets and property, equipment, and software as of December 31, 2023. The termination of this pledge may affect the Company’s asset management and financial leverage.

This summary encapsulates the critical subsequent events that may influence the Company's financial position and operational strategy moving forward.

metadata.title_summary:
title: NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Subsequent Events from the Notes to the Consolidated and Combined Financial Statements**

This summary encapsulates the key developments and transactions that occurred subsequent to the reporting period, reflecting significant events impacting the financial position and operations of the Company.

1. **Merger Transaction**: The Company successfully completed a Merger Transaction on February 22, 2024, as outlined in note 1(b). This strategic move is expected to enhance operational synergies and market positioning.

2. **PIPE Investments**: On February 15, 2024, the Group entered into a new Private Investment in Public Equity (PIPE) investment agreement with an investor, as referenced in note 13. This investment is anticipated to bolster the Company’s capital structure and provide additional resources for growth initiatives.

3. **Share Options Granted**: Between January 1, 2024, and March 31, 2024, the Company granted a total of 5,972,760 share options to employees under the 2022 Share Incentive Plan. The options have a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per option, aligning employee incentives with the Company’s performance.

4. **Loans to Related Party**: On March 11, 2024, a subsidiary of the Company agreed to provide unsecured loans totaling GBP 275,000 (approximately US$354,000) to a related party controlled by the Controlling Shareholder. The loans have a term of 5 years and an annual interest rate of 6.95%, reflecting the Company’s ongoing financial relationships with its stakeholders.

5. **Termination of Pledge**: In April 2024, the Company terminated the pledge associated with the land use right valued at US$32,653 and the corporate buildings under construction valued at US$37,287, both of which were recorded as of December 31, 2023. This termination may indicate a shift in the Company’s asset management strategy.

This summary provides a comprehensive overview of the significant events that have transpired post-reporting period, highlighting the Company’s strategic decisions and financial maneuvers that could influence future performance and shareholder value.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has entered into repurchase agreements with a lending institution in connection with its dealers’ wholesale floor-plan financing of vehicles. Pursuant to the agreements, in the event that a dealer declares the intention to file for bankruptcy, and a lender is able to repossess the vehicle and deliver the repossessed vehicle to Lotus Technology, the company is obligated to repurchase the vehicle from the lender at the unpaid balance of the original invoice price for the vehicle. Lotus Technology did not repurchase any vehicles under the repurchase agreements since the inception of the repurchase agreements, and the company believes that, based on historical experience, the likelihood of a material loss pursuant to these repurchase obligations is remote. The total amount financed under the floor financing programs with repurchase obligations was US$110.5 million as of December 31, 2024. Inventories of US$1.2 million were pledged for other short-term financing arrangements as of December 31, 2024. Property, equipment, and software of US$2.7 million were pledged for other short-term financing arrangements as of December 31, 2024. Except for the above, Lotus Technology has not entered into any material financial guarantees or other commitments to guarantee the payment obligations of any third parties. In addition, Lotus Technology has not entered into any derivative contracts that are indexed to its shares and classified as shareholders’ equity or that are not reflected in its consolidated and combined financial statements except for certain call options that Meritz granted to Lotus Technology in November 2023 in connection with the Meritz Investment.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Off-balance Sheets Arrangements 
summary: The company has established repurchase agreements with a lending institution to facilitate wholesale floor-plan financing for its dealers. These agreements stipulate that if a dealer files for bankruptcy and a vehicle is repossessed, the company must repurchase the vehicle at the outstanding balance of the original invoice price. However, since the inception of these agreements, the company has not had to repurchase any vehicles and considers the risk of significant loss from these obligations to be low, based on historical performance. As of December 31, 2024, the total financing under these programs amounted to $110.5 million. Additionally, the company has pledged inventories valued at $1.2 million and property, equipment, and software worth $2.7 million as collateral for various short-term financing arrangements. The company has not undertaken any significant financial guarantees or commitments for third-party obligations and has not engaged in derivative contracts related to its shares, except for some call options linked to the Meritz Investment granted in November 2023. Furthermore, the company does not retain interests in assets transferred to unconsolidated entities that provide credit or market risk support, nor does it hold variable interests in such entities that offer financing or other services.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: On February 22, 2024, the Merger Transaction was consummated as disclosed in note 1(b) of the financial report. PIPE Investments On February 15, 2024, Lotus Technology entered into a new PIPE investment agreement with an investor as disclosed in note 13. (iii) New grants of share options From January 1, 2024 to March 31, 2024, Lotus Technology granted an aggregated number of 5,972,760 share options to employees pursuant to the 2022 Share Incentive Plan, at a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per share option. (iv) Loans to Lotus Technology's related party On March 11, 2024, Lotus Technology's subsidiary and a related party, ultimately controlled by the Controlling Shareholder, entered into a loan agreement, agreeing that Lotus Technology's subsidiary will provide unsecured loans with a total principal amount of GBP 275,000 (equivalent to US$354,000) with a term of 5 years and an annual interest rate of 6.95%. Termination of pledge The pledge of the land use right of US$32,653 included in the operating lease right-of-use assets and corporate buildings under construction of US$37,287 included in property, equipment, and software as of December 31, 2023, was terminated in April 2024.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 30. SUBSEQUENT EVENTS 
summary: On February 22, 2024, the Merger Transaction was successfully completed, following the finalization of a new PIPE (Private Investment in Public Equity) investment agreement on February 15, 2024. PIPE investments allow companies to quickly raise capital by selling equity at a discount to institutional investors, hedge funds, and private equity firms, providing liquidity and potential upside as stock prices rise post-investment. From January 1 to March 31, 2024, the Company awarded 5,972,760 share options to employees under the 2022 Share Incentive Plan, with a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per option, aimed at aligning employee interests with those of shareholders.

Additionally, on March 11, 2024, a subsidiary linked to a related party controlled by the Controlling Shareholder entered into a loan agreement to provide unsecured loans totaling GBP 275,000 (approximately US$354,000) at an interest rate of 6.95% over five years. Loans to related parties necessitate careful documentation, disclosure, and compliance with regulations to avoid conflicts of interest and ensure transparency. Furthermore, a pledge related to land use rights valued at US$32,653 and corporate buildings under construction worth US$37,287 was terminated in April 2024, releasing the pledged assets from obligations.
